Why Does It Have To Be Beautiful?

Over the course of existence, mankind has strived for beauty. Through art, craft and design we have aspired to make our existence beautiful and meaningful. Adding beauty to everything we do, see, use, create seems to be an evolutionary purpose of mankind. Beauty, not just in its physical form, but also at a deeper level.

Isn’t it beautiful that we care. Isn’t it beautiful that we can love and be loved. Isn’t it beautiful that we can feel.

For us, it all starts and converges here. A moment that we live for sealed with beautiful gift.

Ukiyo is a design studio that creates curated gift packaging.

A moving collage — images transition here. Share the intended dimensions and we'll set up the video / GIF.

Moments we live for

Moments We Live For

Inspired by the idea of “Moments we live for,” the brand draws from the beauty of celebration and the expression of love in its many forms. It stands for cherishing relationships, honoring occasions, and elevating even the smallest gestures through intentional gifting. It’s powerful how gifting is the ultimate tangible expression of an emotion we feel for someone.

Gifting made easy

Gifting Made Easy

Our mission is simple: gifting made easy. By bringing together design-led collections and thoughtful curation, Ukiyo takes care of all milestones and celebrations. With a vision to create a world that celebrates relationships, human connection, and occasions, Ukiyo makes every gift feel beautiful, effortless, and special.

In every detail

Design Philosophy

We believe gift packaging makes a gift look like one. It should carry joy, emotion, and the spirit of a moment worth celebrating. It should feel relevant to the occasion, the season, or the milestone it is made for — brought to life through thoughtful, curated design.

But it’s never just about the pattern. It’s also about function and experience. How does it open? Is there a matching gift tag? Does the bow add to the sense of delight? Every detail matters in making the act of gifting feel more meaningful.

Dots. Stripes. Hearts. Bows. Flowers. They give us joy. A design language we speak — synonymous with joy.
